commands.review_summary:
The PR moves `retireSessionMcpRuntime()` earlier in gateway session cleanup so MCP runtime retirement happens before waiting for the old embedded agent run to end.

PR surface: Source +16. Total +16 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can return from the session reset/delete timeout path without retiring the MCP runtime. I did not run the live WSL2/LadybugDB file-locking MCP repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer lifecycle judgment for drained, force-cleared, or lease-deferred MCP retirement semantics.

target.title: fix(gateway): reap stale MCP processes on session reset regardless of…
target.body:
Cherry-picked from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/92696 (author: lzyyzznl)

## Summary

Moves `retireSessionMcpRuntime()` call BEFORE `waitForEmbeddedAgentRunEnd()` in gateway session cleanup. Previously, MCP processes were only cleaned up inside the `if (ended)` branch -- if the agent run didn't terminate within the 15s grace window, MCP processes would leak, holding SQLite/LadybirdDB file locks and blocking the next session.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: MCP server processes leaking across session boundaries when agent run doesn't terminate within the 15-second grace window during session reset/delete.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v24.13.1, pnpm 11.2.2, latest main with the fix applied

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Branch based on latest `origin/main` with the 16-line fix applied
  2. `pnpm build` -- verified
  3. `pnpm test src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts` -- all session reset cleanup tests pass

- **Evidence after fix**:
  - `retireSessionMcpRuntime` now called unconditionally before the drain wait
  - Both session reset and session delete paths benefit (shared helper)
  - All gateway session reset tests pass
  - Code change: +16 lines in 1 file

- **Observed result after fix**: MCP runtime is retired immediately after aborting the old agent run, regardless of drain timeout.

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway with active MCP connections during session reset timeout.

- **Proof limitations or environment constraints**: Local build/test verification on Linux.

---
*Cherry-pick of #92696 | Rebased on latest main | Fixes #92569*
target.author: zhanxingxin1998
